title: Pan Seared Tunisian Snow Peas Tagine
description: A traditional Tunisian dish featuring charred and gooey snow peas in a flavorful tagine sauce, perfect for a hangover cure or a buddhist-temple-friendly meal.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cumin and coriander and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the snow peas and cook until they are tender and slightly charred.
In a small bowl, whisk together the vegetable broth, tomato paste, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the sauce over the snow peas and bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 10 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the snow peas are coated.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro.
